# Fixture for Step 3.4 of ELOT-GPTEL-PLAN.org.
#
# Demonstrates a class of OMN error that ELOT's per-axiom lint cannot
# catch but ROBOT does: an object property used where a class is
# expected.  Here, `ex:hasPart` is declared as an ObjectProperty, then
# referenced as the superclass of `ex:Foo` (`SubClassOf :: ex:hasPart`).
#
# Expectations:
#   - elot_lint                              : OK (the CURIE is declared,
#                                              not an annotation property,
#                                              and Manchester syntax for
#                                              the value parses cleanly).
#   - elot_omn_validate FILE nil             : ERROR (ROBOT convert
#                                              rejects an ObjectProperty
#                                              in class position).

#+title: Object property in class position
#+author: ELOT test suite

* op-in-class
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class
:ELOT-context-type: ontology
:ELOT-context-localname: op-in-class
:header-args:omn: :tangle ./op-in-class.omn :noweb yes
:ELOT-default-prefix: ex
:END:

** Prefixes
:PROPERTIES:
:prefixdefs: yes
:END:

#+name: prefix-table
| prefix | uri                                         |
|--------+---------------------------------------------|
| ex:    | http://example.org/                         |
| owl:   | http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#              |
| rdf:   | http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns# |
| rdfs:  | http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#       |
| skos:  | http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#        |

** op-in-class ontology (ex:op-in-class ex:op-in-class/0.0)
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-ontology-declaration
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:

 - rdfs:comment :: Deliberately misuses an object property in class position.

** Classes
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-class-hierarchy
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:
*** Foo (ex:Foo)
 - rdfs:label :: Foo
 - SubClassOf :: ex:hasPart

** Object properties
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-object-property-hierarchy
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:
*** hasPart (ex:hasPart)
 - rdfs:label :: has part

** Data properties
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-data-property-hierarchy
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:

** Annotation properties
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-annotation-property-hierarchy
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:

** Individuals
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-individuals
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:

** Datatypes
:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       op-in-class-datatypes
:resourcedefs: yes
:END:
